title: memset should be avoided to delete sensitive data
description: "memset" should not be used to delete sensitive dataAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalsecurityVulnerabilitycweIf a buffer contains sensitive data, such as passwords or access tokens, it is good practice to overwrite the buffer before releasing the memory. This ensures that the sensitive data is not available when that memory is reallocated. Thememsetfunction is commonly used for this purpose.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThe C language specification allows the compiler to remove unnecessary code during the optimization phase.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Memset should be avoided to delete sensitive data. Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.
